rulings.Case Title: WENDY SHARP VS. A STAR HOLDINGS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, Line 6. RESPONDENTS A STAR HOLDINGS LLC, AND PETER ACWORTH's Motion For Protective Order. The unopposed motion for protective order is granted. All post-judgment discovery against Respondents is stayed while the appeal from the judgment is pending. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN RUTH KASCH ET AL VS. OWL, INC., DBA OWL TRANSPORTATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant Laster Walker And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFFS KATHRYN KASCH, KYLE NEWPORT, AND MARIA NEWPORT's Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant Laster Walker And For Sanctions. By companion order today, the court has largely granted plaintiff Kathryn Ruth Kasch's motion to compel further production of documents from Owl, Inc. The present motion concerns Kasch's request for documents from Laster Walker, an individual whom Kasch alleges uses Owl Inc. as his alter ego. The court recognizes that production by Walker implicates privacy concerns that production by Owl Inc. does not; that parties seeking to compel production must articulate specific facts showing good cause justifying this discovery (CCP 2031.310(b)(1)), but that a party should not have to be able to prove her case to be able to obtain discovery to prove her case. Alter ego liability is the exception, not the rule. The court's discovery order today is intended to provide Kasch with initial discovery and permit her to make a further showing of specific facts if supported. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN RUTH KASCH ET AL VS. OWL, INC., DBA OWL TRANSPORTATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Requests For Production Of Documents From Defendant Owl Inc And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFFS KATHRYN KASCH, KYLE NEWPORT, AND MARIA NEWPORT's Motion To Compel Further Response To Requests For Production Of Documents From Defendant Owl Inc And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Kathryn Ruth Kasch seeks documents from defendant Owl, Inc. that she contends are relevant to her alter ego allegations. Specifically, Kasch has alleged that Owl, Inc. is the alter ego of its owner, defendant Laster Walker. Owl has largely refused to produce documents responsive to Kasch's RFPs, citing privacy and relevance objections. It has permitted Kasch's counsel to view a financial document purportedly to show its corporate independence and has produced a heavily redacted set of corporate minutes. Owl has not substantiated any claim that responding to Kasch's discovery requests in full would subject it to particular burdens. (See Williams v. Superior Court (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 549 ["An objection based upon burden must be sustained by evidence showing the quantum of work required."] [internal quotation marks omitted].) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JOSIE ACOSTA VS. CCSF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, Line 3. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND XIAO HUANG's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. The City and County of San Francisco demurs to plaintiff Josie Acosta's second cause of action for negligence per se, as pleaded in her First Amended Complaint (FAC), on grounds that it does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. The negligence per se doctrine . . . creates a presumption of negligence if four elements are established: (1) the defendant violated a statute, ordinance, or regulation of a public entity; (2) the violation proximately caused death or injury to person or property; (3) the death or injury resulted from an occurrence of the nature of which the statute, ordinance, or regulation was designed to prevent; and (4) the person suffering the death or the injury to his person or property was one of the class of persons for whose protection the statute, ordinance, or regulation was adopted. The first two elements are questions of fact, while the latter two are questions of law." (Spates v. Dameron Hospital Assn. (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 208, 218 [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].) The court overrules the demurrer. Acosta alleges that she is a disabled person who tripped over a ramp that should not have been extended while she was trying to board a bus. (FAC para. 11.) She alleges the bus driver failed to provide required assistance to her as a disabled passenger, which resulted in her fall. (FAC para. 16.) She alleges that defendants violated Civil Code sections protecting disabled people and that she was in the class of persons protected by those laws. (FAC para. 30.) These allegations on their face satisfy the factual elements of negligence per se. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC. VS. PHILIP BOYNTON MINER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, Line 4. PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. The court denies the motion for assignment of rights, restraining order, and turnover order without prejudice.
